title: Why CarMax Stock Rebounded on Thursday
author: Eric Volkman
description: CarMax stock surged 13% on Thursday following positive analyst upgrades, despite initial negative investor reaction to its Q1 fiscal 2027 earnings. The company beat top and bottom-line expectations with 6% year-over-year top-line growth. Key upgrades included Stephens analyst Jeff Lick raising his rating to overweight with a $66 price target (up from $43), and Baird raising its target to $55 from $48.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rebounded 13% on Thursday driven by multiple analyst upgrades and positive post-earnings reassessments. Stephens upgraded to overweight with significant price target increase ($43 to $66), Baird raised target to $55, and company demonstrated strong 6% YoY top-line growth in challenging auto sales environment.
While JPMorgan raised its CarMax price target by $1 to $38, it maintained its underweight (sell) recommendation, indicating a mixed and cautious outlook on the stock.

title: Why CarMax Stock Plummeted Today
author: Keith Noonan
description: CarMax stock fell approximately 9% despite reporting better-than-expected fiscal Q1 results with EPS of $1.31 and revenue of $8.01 billion. The decline was driven by forward guidance that came in below market expectations, combined with broader market weakness.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock plummeted 9% despite beating earnings and revenue expectations. The decline was primarily driven by forward guidance falling short of market targets, indicating investor disappointment with future outlook despite strong current quarter performance. Year-over-year EPS also declined 5.1%.

title: Small Caps Near Record Highs, Robinhood Rallies 12%: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks traded higher midday Wednesday with semiconductor equipment names and small caps leading gains ahead of Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first interest-rate decision. The Russell 2000 jumped 1.2% to a record high. Applied Materials surged 7% after Citi raised its price target, while Robinhood rallied 12.2% on strong trading volumes. Exchange operators fell sharply after Kalshi's Bitcoin futures approval raised competitive concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 7% after Citi raised price target to $710 from $550 and reiterated Buy rating, citing durable AI-driven demand for wafer-fab equipment
Jumped 12.2% after Deutsche Bank raised price target to $105 from $98, citing record June trading volumes and workforce optimization
Surged 13.3% on reports that OpenAI is evaluating capacity at the company's Project Matador AI power campus with potential for major commercial agreement
Dropped 5.8% after analysts flagged Kalshi's approval to offer Bitcoin perpetual futures as a competitive threat to incumbent venues
Fell 4.4% due to competitive concerns from Kalshi's Bitcoin futures approval affecting exchange operators
Dropped 7.6% as peer exchange operators were hammered by competitive threats from Kalshi's Bitcoin futures approval
Fell 7.0% despite beating earnings expectations, as profit slipped year-over-year from $1.38 to $1.31 adjusted EPS

title: CarMax Warns Lower Vehicle Margins Will Persist
author: Lekha Gupta
description: CarMax reported Q1 FY2027 earnings and revenue above analyst expectations, with adjusted EPS of $1.31 vs. consensus of $0.94 and revenue of $8.01B vs. expected $7.41B. However, the stock fell 5.64% in premarket trading as investors focused on declining margins. Gross profit per retail used vehicle fell $230 to $2,177, and the company warned that margin pressure will persist as it prioritizes sales growth over profitability through pricing adjustments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite beating earnings and revenue expectations, the stock declined 5.64% due to investor concerns over deteriorating vehicle margins, declining gross profit per unit ($230 decrease), and management's guidance indicating continued margin pressure as the company prioritizes volume growth over profitability.

title: Stock Market: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down Today?
author: Eva Mathew
description: The S&P 500 fell 0.57% on Tuesday as investors rotated from chip stocks to cyclical sectors. However, Polymarket traders predict a 78% probability the index will open higher Wednesday, supported by easing Middle East tensions and expectations of unchanged Fed rates at the FOMC meeting. S&P 500 futures were modestly higher early Wednesday, up 0.28%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CarMax is mentioned as scheduled to report earnings before Wednesday's opening bell, but no specific performance data or outlook is provided in the article. The mention is purely informational without positive or negative context.
